vocab.meaning: một vấn đề thuộc về nguyên tắc
vocab.example: For me, wearing a watch is a point of principle; it shows that I value other people's time as much as my own.

vocab.meaning: tránh xa cái gì đó (tránh xao nhãng từ điện thoại)
vocab.example: I wear a watch to steer clear of the digital distractions that inevitably pop up whenever I check my phone for the time.

vocab.meaning: kiểm soát chặt chẽ (kiểm soát lịch trình)
vocab.example: Having a timepiece on my wrist helps me keep a tight rein on my daily schedule, especially during back-to-back meetings.

vocab.meaning: đón nhận kết quả tốt (từ việc rèn luyện tính kỷ luật thời gian)
vocab.example: Cultivating the habit of wearing a watch eventually bears fruit in the form of better productivity and a more organized life.

vocab.meaning: một lựa chọn an toàn/chắc chắn
vocab.example: In a formal interview, wearing a classic watch is a safe bet to project an image of reliability and professional maturity.

vocab.word: not give someone the time of day
vocab.ipa: /nɒt ɡɪv ˈsʌm.wʌn ðə taɪm əv deɪ/
vocab.meaning: không thèm đoái hoài/coi thường (nghĩa bóng: đồng hồ giúp ta không thô lỗ khi cần xem giờ)
vocab.example: Glancing at a watch is discreet; checking a phone can make it look like you won't give the person you're with the time of day.

vocab.meaning: lấp đầy khoảng trống (giữa phong cách và tính tiện dụng)
vocab.example: A high-quality watch bridges the gap between functional utility and sartorial elegance perfectly.

vocab.meaning: điều thiết yếu/phổ biến (tính đúng giờ là điều thiết yếu)
vocab.example: In the corporate world, punctuality is the order of the day, making a reliable watch an absolute necessity.

vocab.meaning: với mục đích/ý định làm gì
vocab.example: I wear a watch with a view to reducing my overall screen time and becoming more present in my physical surroundings.
That a watch is more convenient than a phone in a formal exam or a quick meeting is beyond all dispute.
